authoritative_sources.title: Interagency Guidance on Third-Party Relationships: Risk Management
authoritative_sources.publisher: Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System, Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation, and Office of the Comptroller of the Currency
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary U.S. banking-agency guidance governing risk-management practices across all stages of the life cycle of third-party relationships, making it authoritative for bank-fintech partnership-operations boundaries and lifecycle design.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Relationship lifecycle, monitoring, escalation, and termination gating
used_for: scope boundaries for active partner oversight

authoritative_sources.title: Third-Party Risk Management: A Guide for Community Banks
authoritative_sources.publisher: Office of the Comptroller of the Currency
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official operating guide tied directly to active third-party relationship handling, including monitoring, customer complaints, issue escalation, and contract-termination planning relevant to fintech partnership operations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Ongoing monitoring, complaint review, and closure or transition readiness
used_for: partner scorecard and complaint artifact expectations

authoritative_sources.title: Conducting Due Diligence on Financial Technology Companies: A Guide for Community Banks
authoritative_sources.publisher: Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System, Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation, and Office of the Comptroller of the Currency
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official interagency fintech guide identifying the evidence banks should collect and retain on security, subcontractors, incidents, audits, record ownership, and transition rights in fintech relationships.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Evidence collection, remediation tracking, and partner transition safeguards
used_for: failure-mode definition when records or rights are missing

authoritative_sources.title: Compliance Management Review Examination Procedures
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official CFPB examination procedures defining monitoring, service-provider coverage, complaint recording and resolution, corrective action, and escalation of consumer-harm issues in supervised consumer-finance operations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Complaint handling, corrective action, and service-provider oversight
used_for: escalation triggers for complaints and compliance concerns

authoritative_sources.title: Third Parties in the ACH Network
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Recognized ACH network operating guidance that defines Third-Party Sender and Nested Third-Party Sender roles, annual audit and risk-assessment obligations, relationship agreements, and ACH chain-of-responsibility terms used in embedded-finance partnership operations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: ACH role classification, agreement validation, and payment-operations evidence review
used_for: ACH role and agreement artifact mapping

authoritative_sources.title: PCI Data Security Standard (PCI DSS)
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Recognized payment-card control standard defining baseline technical and operational requirements for processors, issuers, and service providers involved in payment card processing, which constrains partner evidence handling and escalation.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Security evidence handling, access control, and card-data-sensitive escalation
used_for: completion gating for security-sensitive partner items

authoritative_sources.title: The NIST Cybersecurity Framework (CSF) 2.0
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Recognized U.S. public control framework for cybersecurity risk governance, communication, and prioritization, directly useful for partner incident, remediation, access, and change-management evidence expectations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Incident, remediation, and control-governance evidence handling
used_for: change and access control escalation logic

workflow_stages.name: Accepted intake and partner-context validation
workflow_stages.description: Validate partner identity, program or product context, sponsor-bank or processor role, governing agreement reference, work-item type, and evidence sufficiency before any progression recommendation.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Partner monitoring and evidence normalization
workflow_stages.description: Normalize scorecards, KPIs, KRIs, complaint records, issue logs, audit findings, control evidence, and change or incident artifacts into a single traceable partner-operations packet.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Execution, exception handling, and rework progression
workflow_stages.description: Move work through queued, under-review, blocked, escalated, awaiting-partner, rework, ready-for-closure, and completed states based on documented issue status, owner actions, and evidence.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Consumer-impact, compliance, and security escalation
workflow_stages.description: Escalate when complaint trends, service-provider failures, card-data issues, ACH role ambiguity, or security or regulatory concerns change whether the partner work item may safely proceed.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Completion confirmation and transition readiness
workflow_stages.description: Issue completion or closure records only when remediation evidence, owner signoff, system updates, and any required transition or data-ownership steps are present.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: Partner inventory, program descriptors, partner tiering, and relationship maps
Executed agreements, statements of work, operating addenda, RACI documents, and approval matrices
Partner scorecards, KPI and KRI packs, governance meeting notes, issue logs, and remediation plans
Consumer complaint logs, support escalations, corrective-action records, and service-provider follow-up artifacts
Incident tickets, root-cause analyses, postmortems, security assessments, and change requests or approvals
ACH Third-Party Sender, Nested TPS, audit, risk-assessment, and origination-agreement artifacts where applicable
PCI, access, incident, and control assessment evidence plus closure or transition records
systems_of_record: Third-party risk management or GRC platform
Partner portal, processor dashboard, or sponsor-bank reporting portal
Security assessment repository and audit-evidence store

allowed: Classify requests as in scope, blocked, or out of scope based on fintech partnership-operations ownership.
Validate minimum tenant facts, authoritative records, and source context required for safe work progression.
Prepare execution status updates, exception notes, partner-operations summaries, and completion records tied to named artifacts.
Advance, hold, rework, or complete work based on documented lifecycle state and evidence.
escalate: Missing or contradictory contract, scorecard, complaint, incident, or approval evidence materially changes the outcome.
Legal interpretation, compliance signoff, sponsor-bank approval, product approval, or security-incident-command ownership is required.
ACH role definitions, payment-intermediary obligations, or customer-impact concerns change whether the work may safely proceed.
Public baseline guidance, contract language, and tenant or sponsor-bank instructions conflict on progression or closure.
refuse: Requests to fabricate approvals, closure evidence, complaint resolution, or remediation status.
Requests that require legal, compliance, executive, sponsor-bank, commercial, or security-command authority outside the lane.
Requests to mark work complete while closure evidence, corrective action, or required owner signoff is missing.

domain_failure_modes: Advancing or closing partner work without a governing agreement reference, owner map, and named evidence bundle.
Treating generic vendor-management records as sufficient for sponsor-bank, processor, TPS, or embedded-finance partnership work.
Ignoring complaint, consumer-harm, or security-sensitive indicators embedded in partner issue records.
Treating ACH role ownership or contractual obligations as interchangeable when TPS, Nested TPS, ODFI, or processor roles materially differ.
Closing remediation or incident work without system updates, owner signoff, and corrective-action evidence.
Applying public baseline guidance when tenant contract terms, sponsor-bank procedures, or processor rules impose stricter holds or approvals.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Execution exception that requires rework or escalation
Request to mark work complete without evidence of completion

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant-specific definition of which committee, sponsor-bank delegate, or executive role must sign off on material partner changes or closure for higher-risk programs
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_path: Require tenant approval-matrix retrieval and hold completion or progression whenever the controlling approver is not identifiable from runtime facts.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Whether the controlling system of record for open partner issues is the internal GRC or ticketing platform, the sponsor-bank reporting portal, or the processor partner portal for a given program
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_path: Require tenant source-of-record precedence rules and block high-impact state changes until the controlling record source is confirmed.
